title: MBA in Media Management
content: The MBA in Media Management is designed to prepare future-ready professionals who can think strategically, communicate creatively, and manage media businesses in a digital-first world. The programme combines management principles with media industry practice so that students learn not only how media works, but also how to lead media-driven growth, create value for audiences, and respond to changing technologies. It emphasises a balance of business thinking, creative execution, ethical responsibility, and technological competence. The philosophy of the programme is rooted in the belief that media today is both a cultural force and a business ecosystem, and successful professionals must understand both dimensions. Students are trained to work across digital marketing, advertising, content production, automation, AI, storytelling, and client relationships with a strong focus on innovation, adaptability, and professional excellence. Why Students Should Join Bachelor Degree with Minimum 50% aggregate marks Students having MAT / XAT / CMAT / NMAT / GMAT/ CUET Score will be given preference in merit The programme opens career paths across media, marketing, advertising, and digital communication sectors. Graduates can work in roles such as: Graduates may find opportunities in advertising agencies, digital marketing firms, media houses, OTT platforms, publishing companies, production houses, corporate communication teams, public relations agencies, and in-house brand departments. The programme also supports entrepreneurial pathways for those interested in launching content, marketing, or media consulting ventures. Growth in the Global Market The global media and digital marketing market continues to expand because brands increasingly depend on online platforms, data-driven communication, and personalised content. Demand is rising for professionals who can combine marketing strategy with content creation, audience insight, campaign analytics, and AI-enabled production. Media organisations worldwide are investing in automation, performance marketing, creator ecosystems, streaming platforms, and cross-channel storytelling. This creates strong demand for graduates who understand both creative and managerial dimensions of media. Global employers value professionals who can adapt to new tools, work across platforms, and manage campaigns in fast-changing environments. As media becomes more integrated with technology and analytics, the need for management-trained media professionals is expected to grow further across international markets. 01 Domain Knowledge: Apply core and advanced concepts of media management, digital marketing, advertising, content creation, and client engagement to professional practice. 02 Critical Thinking: Analyse media and marketing situations using logic, evidence, and contextual understanding to make informed decisions. 03 Design Thinking: Develop audience-centred and innovative media solutions by applying creative, empathetic, and iterative thinking. 04 Problem Solving: Identify complex media, marketing, and communication problems and propose effective, practical, and ethical solutions. 05 Team Work: Collaborate effectively in multidisciplinary teams to plan, execute, and evaluate media and marketing projects. 06 Safety at Workspace: Follow workplace safety, digital security, and responsible professional practices in media and marketing environments. 07 Effective Communication: Communicate ideas, strategies, and insights clearly and persuasively through oral, written, visual, and digital formats. 08 Global Citizenship: Demonstrate awareness of global media trends, intercultural sensitivity, and responsible participation in diverse professional contexts. 09 Project Management: Plan, organise, implement, and monitor media and marketing projects within time, budget, and quality constraints. 10 Media and Marketing Ethics: Apply ethical, legal, and socially responsible principles in content creation, promotion, data use, and client communication. 11 Environment and Sustainability: Integrate environmentally responsible and sustainable practices into media operations, campaigns, and organisational decisions. 12 Self-directed and Life-long Learning: Engage in continuous, self-directed learning to adapt to evolving technologies, tools, and industry expectations. 01 Apply core management, media, marketing, and communication concepts to solve professional problems in media organisations. 02 Analyse media markets, audiences, platforms, and business environments using qualitative and quantitative methods. 03 Design and execute digital marketing, media planning, content, and communication strategies for organisational goals. 04 Use digital tools, analytics, automation, and emerging technologies effectively in media and marketing practice. 05 Create original, audience-focused, and platform-specific content for diverse media and business contexts. 06 Demonstrate ethical, legal, social, and environmental responsibility in media, marketing, and communication decisions. 07 Communicate clearly and professionally with clients, teams, stakeholders, and audiences across formats and media. 08 Work effectively in teams, lead collaborative projects, and manage creative and strategic workflows. 09 Evaluate media and marketing performance using data, feedback, and research to improve outcomes and ROI. 10 Exhibit innovation, adaptability, entrepreneurial thinking, and lifelong learning in a changing media landscape
